mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in >55% of observations following a rate decline, or rolling Rank IC falls below 0.02 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals sudden labor market deterioration, triggering immediate equity risk-off flows and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in >60% of triggered instances, or the signal fails to separate positive from negative return distributions (KS test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer demand growth, which drives corporate earnings revisions and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields a negative average 60-day forward return, or the interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.05) in a multivariate regression over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices reflect expanding manufacturing capacity and output, which translates to higher corporate profit margins and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative 20-day forward returns during early-cycle expansions, or the correlation between INDPRO growth and SPX returns turns negative (r < -0.1) in this regime.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real gross domestic product confirms macroeconomic expansion, supporting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days following data release.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following positive GDP prints are negative in >50% of cases, or the signal predictive power vanishes (Rank IC < 0.01) across all tested windows.

mechanism_narrative: When consumer price inflation surpasses a 2.5% annual threshold, markets anticipate aggressive monetary tightening, which compresses equity multiples and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 60-day returns in >65% of threshold-crossing events, or the inflation threshold fails to predict return direction (logistic regression coefficient insignificant at 95% confidence).

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in total nonfarm payrolls and declines in the unemployment rate confirm a strengthening labor market, which boosts consumer spending expectations and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal yields negative 20-day forward returns, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in retail sales signals weakening consumer demand, which forces downward earnings revisions and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Retail sales declines are followed by positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in >55% of observations during late-cycle expansions, or the signal Sharpe ratio falls below 0.2 in walk-forward validation.
